sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Crystal Palace are in strong form, remaining unbeaten in their last 5 matches with 3 wins and 2 draws, showcasing their attacking prowess with players like Eberechi Eze in top form. Wolverhampton have had mixed results, with 3 wins and 2 losses in their last 5 matches, but they have shown capability to score, led by Matheus Cunha.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Crystal Palace have not lost, with a 3-1 win at home and a 2-2 draw away against Wolverhampton. Recent encounters often see both teams scoring and matches with over 2.5 goals, indicating a trend of open, high-scoring games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Crystal Palace (12th, 49 points) have outperformed Wolverhampton (14th, 41 points) in the league standings, with better defensive stats (48 goals conceded vs. 64 for Wolves). Palace also create more big chances (101 vs. Wolves' stats not fully provided), suggesting a slight edge in attack.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Crystal Palace will miss key defender Marc Guéhi, potentially weakening their backline, but Eze’s form could be decisive. Wolverhampton are without Hee-Chan Hwang, impacting their forward line, though Cunha remains a significant threat.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Considering Palace’s unbeaten run, home advantage at Selhurst Park, and historical edge in head-to-head matches, they are favored to win. However, Wolves’ ability to score in recent games suggests a competitive match, likely with goals on both sides.
